description: Multi-phase traffic capture and analysis system: transparent proxy (Phase 1), soft router (Phase 2), AI analysis (Phase 3), and mock API generation (Phase 4). PC acts as phone gateway, decrypts HTTPS, classifies by app, infers API semantics, and generates mock backends.

userStories.title: Database schema for traffic storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need traffic data stored in SQLite so it persists across sessions and can be queried.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite WAL mode database at ~/.proxybot/proxybot.db
Tables: http_requests, dns_queries, devices, app_tags
http_requests: id, timestamp, method, scheme, host, path, req_headers (JSON), req_body (BLOB), resp_status, resp_headers (JSON), resp_body (BLOB), duration_ms, device_id, app_tag
dns_queries: id, timestamp, query_name, query_type, response_ips (JSON), device_id
devices: id, mac_address, name, created_at, last_seen_at
app_tags: id, app_name, domain_rule (JSON)
Indexes on host, timestamp, device_id for fast lookup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented with rusqlite in db.rs. Schema includes http_requests, dns_queries, devices, app_tags tables with proper indexes.

userStories.title: Root CA generation and storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a self-signed root CA generated on first launch so HTTPS MITM can work.
acceptanceCriteria: On first launch, check ~/.proxybot/ca.pem — if missing, generate RSA 2048 root CA using rcgen
Root CA stored as PEM at ~/.proxybot/ca.pem with creation timestamp in ~/.proxybot/ca.meta.json
CA is used to sign dynamic leaf certificates per destination
CA PEM is exportable for phone installation (download button in UI)
CA can be regenerated; existing leaf certs remain valid within a session
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in cert.rs. CA stored as PEM string (ca.pem) with metadata (ca.meta.json). CertManager uses Mutex for interior mutability on CA fields. Download button copies PEM to clipboard. Regenerate clears host cert cache.

userStories.title: macOS pf transparent proxy setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IP forwarding configured on macOS so all phone traffic routes through the proxy.
acceptanceCriteria: On first launch with admin privileges, write pf anchor to /etc/pf.anchors/proxybot
Anchor redirects TCP 80/443 to localhost:8080
pf.conf is updated to load the anchor
pf is enabled via pfctl
Admin prompt appears only on first launch, not on subsequent runs
On uninstall/revert, pf anchor is removed and pf restarted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: pf anchor setup fully implemented in pf.rs. Admin prompt optimization added: is_pf_enabled() check prevents redundant prompts when pf is already active.

userStories.title: Local DNS server with query logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DNS server on port 53 that logs all queries so I can correlate DNS lookups with connections.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent starts a UDP/TCP DNS server on port 53
Server forwards all queries to upstream DNS (configurable, default 8.8.8.8:53)
All queries are logged to dns_queries table with timestamp and response IPs
Server responds with correct A/AAAA records from upstream response
DNS server starts automatically with the agent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DNS server on port 5300 (pf redirects 53->5300). Logs queries to dns_queries table with timestamp and response IPs. Uses DnsState::with_db() constructor to pass DbState. Response IPs extracted via parse_response_ips() which parses A records from DNS response.

userStories.title: TLS MITM with dynamic leaf certificates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the proxy to decrypt HTTPS traffic so I can inspect request and response bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: Proxy listens on localhost:8080 for HTTP and HTTPS (CONNECT tunneling)
For each HTTPS destination, dynamically generate a leaf X509 certificate signed by root CA
rustls handles TLS termination on the proxy side; original traffic is forwarded to upstream
Leaf certificates are cached per destination for session reuse
Undecryptable traffic (certificate pinning, etc.) is logged but does not crash the proxy
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fully implemented in proxy.rs. handle_https_connect performs MITM with TlsAcceptor (browser-facing) and TlsConnector (upstream-facing). CertManager::generate_host_cert creates RSA leaf certs signed by CA. host_certs HashMap provides caching. Falls back to raw TCP tunnel on failures.

userStories.title: TUN/VPN mode fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a VPN fallback mode so Android 7+ and iOS devices without MDM can still use the proxy.
acceptanceCriteria: When pf/netsh redirect is unavailable, agent falls back to TUN interface mode
tokio-tun creates a userspace TUN device on macOS/Windows
TUN captures all device traffic and feeds it to the proxy pipeline
VPN mode is selectable in UI when pf setup fails
TUN mode does not require kernel-level changes or admin beyond VPN profile installation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in tun.rs using native macOS PF_SYSTEM socket for utun interface creation. TUN interface configured with ifconfig (IP 10.0.0.1/24) and routing via route command. UI added in App.tsx with TUN/VPN mode section.

userStories.title: Traffic dashboard — request list with host grouping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see live HTTP/WebSocket traffic in a web UI so I can filter and inspect requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Web UI served at localhost:19999
Scrollable request list: method, URL, status, timestamp, size
Click a request to expand: full headers (req+resp), query params, body with JSON/HTML formatter
Host filter: dropdown groups by host, text input for keyword filter
WebSocket frames shown as bidirectional events with direction indicator
Images in responses render as thumbnails
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented traffic dashboard with scrollable request list, expandable request details (headers, body, params), host filter dropdown, keyword search, JSON/HTML body formatter, and image thumbnail rendering. Response bodies and headers are now captured. WebSocket frame infrastructure added (is_websocket flag, ws_frames field) with UI ready to display when captured.

userStories.title: App classification by DNS correlation and SNI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want traffic automatically tagged by app (WeChat, Douyin, Alipay) so I can filter by source.
acceptanceCriteria: DNS query log correlated with subsequent TCP/TLS connections by timing (within 5s window)
Domain rules: WeChat: *.weixin.qq.com, *.wechat.com, *.qq.com; Douyin: *.douyin.com, *.tiktokv.com; Alipay: *.alipay.com, *.alipaycdn.com
SNI field inspected as secondary classification signal
Each request row in dashboard shows app tag badge
App classification is extensible via rules file at ~/.proxybot/app_rules.json
Unclassified traffic shows 'unknown' tag
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented SNI extraction from TLS ClientHello in proxy.rs. DNS queries now store app_name and app_icon. DnsState::correlate_app() uses 5s window for DNS-to-request correlation. App rules loadable from ~/.proxybot/app_rules.json with serde support. Default rules for WeChat, Douyin, Alipay with domain suffix matching.

userStories.title: YAML rule engine with hot reload
userStories.description: As a user, I want routing rules loaded from YAML so traffic can be direct/proxied/blocked per domain/IP.
acceptanceCriteria: Rules loaded from ~/.proxybot/rules/*.yaml on startup
Support keywords: DOMAIN, DOMAIN-SUFFIX, IP-CIDR, GEOIP, RULE-SET
Actions: DIRECT, PROXY, REJECT
File watcher triggers hot-reload within 2 seconds of file save
Rules evaluated in order; first match wins
Rule editor UI: create/edit/delete/reorder rules in Web UI
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DNS enhanced layer with DoH/DoT support
userStories.description: As a user, I want the DNS server to support DoH and DoT so encrypted DNS can be used upstream.
acceptanceCriteria: DNS upstream configurable: plain UDP, DoH URL, DoT hostname
Default upstream: https://1.1.1.1/dns-query (DoH)
Local hosts file at ~/.proxybot/hosts merged into DNS responses
Ad-block list (hosts format) loaded from ~/.proxybot/blocklist.txt
Domain-to-DNS-resolution fed to routing engine for policy decisions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Multi-device registry and per-device policies
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage multiple phones independently so each gets its own policy and statistics.
acceptanceCriteria: Devices identified by MAC address; stored in devices table with human-readable name
Web UI shows device list with name, MAC, last seen, byte totals
Each device can be assigned a rule set override
Per-device byte counters (upload/download) updated in real-time
Device topology diagram in Web UI (simple node graph)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Traffic recording and HAR export
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want to record traffic and export it so I can replay against a mock server later.
acceptanceCriteria: All traffic for a session stored in SQLite
Export button generates HAR 1.2 file downloadable from UI
HAR includes: request (url, method, headers, postData), response (status, headers, content), timing
pcap export option for raw packet-level recording
Session can be named and saved for later replay
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Traffic replay with diff view
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want to replay recorded traffic against a local mock and see diffs so I know what the mock is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Select a recorded host → replay all requests against local mock server
Mock server serves recorded responses as fixtures
Diff view shows header and body differences side-by-side
Replay supports variable delay injection between requests
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Traffic normalizer — HTTP to structured schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need HTTP exchanges stored as structured records so AI can analyze them.
acceptanceCriteria: Each HTTP exchange normalized into schema: method, path, query (JSON), request_headers (JSON), request_body (parsed), response_status, response_headers (JSON), response_body (parsed), timing_ms, device_id
Body parser detects JSON, Protobuf (base64), GraphQL (JSON) variants
Normalized records replace raw BLOB storage in http_requests table
FastAPI endpoint at /api/traffic returns paginated normalized records
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Request DAG builder from traffic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need request dependency graph so I understand call ordering and ID passing chains.
acceptanceCriteria: DAG constructed from timestamp ordering plus extracted ID/token references
Token extraction regex: access_token, sessionId, auth_token, id, uid, refresh_token
Edges: request A → request B if B carries a token previously returned by A
DAG stored as adjacency list in SQLite (dag_nodes, dag_edges tables)
FastAPI endpoint at /api/traffic/dag returns DAG as JSON (nodes + edges)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LLM API semantic inference
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the LLM to infer API semantics from a request sequence so I don't have to manually name endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude API called with full session request sequence as context
Prompt: 'Given these HTTP requests, output JSON with: interfaces [{name, method, path, params (semantic description), auth_required}] and modules [{name, description, interface_ids}]'
Output validated as JSON with expected schema before storing
Results stored in inferred_apis table
OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated from inferred APIs at /api/traffic/openapi.yaml
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Evaluator agent for LLM output validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an evaluator to check LLM output consistency so generated specs are reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluator prompt: 'Review the inferred API JSON. Check: all requests are covered, paths are correctly typed, auth chains are consistent, parameter names match request bodies'
Evaluator outputs: {valid: bool, errors: [string], score: float}
If score < 0.8, LLM is re-called with evaluator feedback for up to 2 retries
Final output marked with evaluator score
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: State machine visualizer for auth flows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the login → token → resource lifecycle as a diagram so I understand the app's auth flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth flow extracted from DAG: nodes are session states, edges are authenticated calls
State machine output as Mermaid markdown at /api/traffic/state-machine
Web UI renders Mermaid diagram inline
Anomalous transitions (resource called before login) flagged as warnings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Anomaly detection and privacy scanning
userStories.description: As a security researcher, I want alerts when traffic looks suspicious or private data is being exfiltrated.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-device traffic baseline: 7-day rolling domain/IP frequency profile
New domain or IP not in baseline triggers info-level alert
Privacy scanner regex-matches request/response bodies for: IDFA, phone number (E.164), GPS coordinates (lat/long pairs)
Alerts stored in alerts table with severity (info/warning/critical), device_id, details
Alert badge in dashboard; alert list page with filter by severity
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Mock API code generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want OpenAPI spec converted to a working mock API server so I can replace the real backend.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAPI 3.1 spec → Hono or FastAPI project via template generation
Recorded responses auto-populated as fixture files per endpoint
Mock server supports ordered sequences (response 1, then 2, then 3 for same endpoint)
Conditional responses: match request body field X → return response Y
Mock runs with `docker compose up` including mock-api and postgres
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend React scaffold generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a React frontend scaffold generated from inferred API semantics so I have a working UI to start from.
acceptanceCriteria: LLM generates React + Vite project from OpenAPI spec
Each API endpoint maps to a typed fetch hook (useXxx query/mutation)
Routes inferred from API paths (e.g., GET /user/profile → /user/profile page)
Basic state management (Zustand store) scaffolded per module
Playwright E2E tests auto-generated per route
Generator → Evaluator闭环: generated code replayed against real traffic, diffs auto-correct scaffold
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Vision screenshot UI analyzer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upload a screenshot and have the system infer UI structure so the scaffold matches the real app.
acceptanceCriteria: Screenshot upload UI in web dashboard
Claude Vision API called with prompt: 'Analyze this mobile app screenshot. Output JSON: {components: [{type, text, position, children}]}'
Vision output stored alongside traffic analysis for same app
Component tree used by scaffold generator to refine React component layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: One-click Docker deployment bundle
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one command to produce a deployable Docker Compose so the replica runs standalone.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate button produces docker-compose.yml with services: mock-api, frontend, postgres
Git repo initialized with generated project files
GitHub Actions CI template runs Playwright E2E suite as gate
README.md with run instructions auto-generated
Typecheck passes
